heading: Laboratory-directed research and development programs
label: 6331 Laboratory-directed research and development programs
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 6331 was renumbered .section 8331 of this titleProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , War and National Defense, prior to repeal by .section 2791 of Title 50Pub. L. 119–60, § 3111(b)(1)

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: Government-owned, contractor-operated laboratories that are funded out of funds available to the Department of Energy for national security programs are authorized to carry out laboratory-directed research and development.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: b .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of Energy shall prescribe regulations for the conduct of laboratory-directed research and development at such laboratories.

children.heading: .—Funding
children.label: c .—Funding
children.text: Of the funds provided by the Department of Energy to a national security laboratory for national security activities, the Secretary shall provide a specific amount, of not less than 5 percent and not more than 7 percent of such funds, to be used by the laboratory for laboratory-directed research and development.

children.heading: .—Laboratory-directed Research and Development Defined
children.label: d .—Laboratory-directed Research and Development Defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “laboratory-directed research and development” means research and development work of a creative and innovative nature which, under the regulations prescribed pursuant to subsection (b), is selected by the director of a laboratory for the purpose of maintaining the vitality of the laboratory in defense-related scientific disciplines.
